docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
• mkdocs new [dir-name] - Create a new project.
  • mkdocs serve - Start the live-reloading docs server.
  • mkdocs build - Build the documentation site.
  • mkdocs -h - Print help message and exit.

docs.title: Project layout
docs.text:
mkdocs.yml    # The configuration file.
docs/
    index.md  # The documentation homepage.
    ...       # Other markdown pages, images and other files.

docs.title: Install MkDocs
docs.text:
mkdir mkdocs_root
cd mkdocs_root
python -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
python -m pip install -U mkdocs-material

docs.title: Live preview - Local development only
docs.text:
mkdocs serve

-a <IP:PORT>: Change the server address and port.
-w <path>: Add custom directories or files to watch for changes.
--dirtyreload: Rebuild only changed files for faster updates (ideal for large projects).

docs.title: Build static site
docs.text:
mkdocs build

By default, the output is placed in the ./site/
-d <build-dir>: Change build directory.
--clean to remove stale files from the site directory before rebuilding.

docs.title: Deploy to Github Pages
docs.text:
mkdocs gh-deploy --force

1. Builds your documentation using mkdocs build.
2. Uses ghp-import to commit the built site to the gh-pages branch.
3. Pushes the gh-pages branch to GitHub, triggering GitHub Pages to serve your site.

--force: Force the push to the repository.
--theme [material|mkdocs|readthedocs]: The theme to use when building your documentation.
